summary: Justin Brennan discusses San Diego's unique investment landscape, including the San Diego State student housing market with ADU conversions generating 6.5% cap rates, and explains why San Diego's diverse economy (military, biotech, tech, pharmaceuticals) creates resilience versus boom-bust markets. He also covers multifamily syndication fundamentals and debt leverage for scaling, but the conversation is heavily weighted toward mindset, personal story, and political topics rather than specific deal mechanics.
takeaways: Cost segregation allows 25-30% of property value recognized as year-one loss against personal income, with improved recent rules allowing closer to 40%
San Diego State area student housing (4-6 bed units with ADUs) trading at 6.5% cap rates turn-key with third-party management
Every $1,000/month of NOI boost equals ~$220,000 property value increase using 5.5% cap rate formula

Debt enables 5x effectiveness vs. all-cash: $1.2M all-in cost yields $300K profit at 25% return; using leverage with $200K capital yields same $300K profit at 150% ROI plus scalability to multiple deals
Hard money lenders typically finance 90% of acquisition + 100% of renovation costs at 10-10.5% rates with interest reserves
Deal-by-deal syndication preferred over fund strategy until track record established; projected investor returns of 18-20% annually on multifamily value-add deals

skill_candidates.change: Apply NOI-to-value formula ($1,000 NOI = $220K value at 5.5% cap) to quickly screen San Diego multifamily deals for upside potential
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explicitly teaches the formula and uses it to justify holding a property worth $2.2M when appraised lower because cash flow justified higher valuation

skill_candidates.change: Prioritize San Diego over boom-bust markets; identify markets with diverse, government-backed employment (military, biotech, tech) as recession-resistant
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin explains San Diego's military assets (40% of global Marine training, Pacific Fleet), biotech/pharma, and tech concentration create stable renter base vs. Arizona/Nevada/Texas markets that boom-and-bust

title: From Fear Of Debt To Real Estate Success: How I Built Wealth Through Multifamily Investing!
